2605.21997.title: The Log is the Agent: Event-Sourced Reactive Graphs for Auditable, Forkable Agentic Systems
2605.21997.paper_summary: Most agent frameworks are built around the language model: a conversation loop comes first, then tools, then rules, and finally a logging layer bolted on for observability, with state persisted as retrievable "memory." We describe ActiveGraph, a runtime that inverts this arrangement. The append-only event log is the source of truth; the working graph is a deterministic projection of that log; and behaviors--ordinary functions, classes, LLM-backed routines, or logic attached to typed edges--react to changes in the graph and emit new events. No component instructs another; coordination happens entirely through the shared graph. This single design decision yields three properties that retrieval-and-summarization memory systems do not provide: deterministic replay of any run from its log, cheap forking that branches a run at any event without re-executing the shared prefix, and end-to-end lineage from a high-level goal down to the individual model call that produced each artifact. We present the architecture, a determinism contract that makes replay sound, and a worked diligence example whose full causal structure is reconstructable from the log alone. We discuss--without claiming to demonstrate--why this substrate is unusually well suited to self-improving agents, and how it extends the BabyAGI lineage and prior graph-memory research.
2605.21997.paper_summary_zh: 大多數代理框架都是圍繞語言模型構建的：首先是對話循環，然後是工具，再然後是規則，最後是附加的日誌層以便於觀察，狀態作為可檢索的“記憶”持久化。我們描述了ActiveGraph，一個顛覆這種安排的運行時。僅附加的事件日誌是事實的來源；工作圖是該日誌的確定性投影；而行為——普通函數、類別、基於LLM的例程或附加到類型邊緣的邏輯——對圖中的變化做出反應並發出新事件。沒有任何組件指示另一個組件；協調完全通過共享圖進行。這一單一設計決策產生了三個檢索和總結記憶系統所不提供的特性：從其日誌中確定性重放任何運行、在任何事件處進行便宜的分支而不重新執行共享前綴，以及從高層目標到生成每個工件的單個模型調用的端到端血統。我們展示了架構、一個使重放合理的確定性合約，以及一個完整因果結構可以僅從日誌重建的詳細示例。我們討論——而不聲稱證明——為什麼這個基礎特別適合自我改進的代理，以及它如何擴展BabyAGI的血統和先前的圖形記憶研究。
2605.21997.author: Yohei Nakajima et.al.
